carPage.body: RC car control through NeuraFlow — coming soon.
carPage.title: Car control

control.localBridgeReadyHint: Local bridge connected — you can start the session. Model inference runs directly on your computer.
control.mainBridgeStartHint: Connect the main bridge and start the EEG stream first.

placeholder.body: The vehicle control components are being developed. The model is running and ready.
placeholder.title: Control panel coming soon

control.readyHint: Model is running — you can start the session.
control.startHint: Launch the model first to begin a session.

modelSelector.empty: No trained models available. Complete a training session first.

panel.deployHint: Select a model above to launch it.

noModels.body: Complete a training session to make a model available for deployment.
noModels.title: No trained models

panel.subtitle: Select and launch a trained model before starting control.
panel.title: Model configuration

droneConfig.bciSourceLocalBridgeFootnote: A model trained locally on the user's machine. Inference runs on the local bridge — no deployment through the app required.
droneConfig.controlTileSubtitle: Pick a mode before you configure the sections below.
droneConfig.introSubtitle: Set up your flight options and then the sections below. When you are ready, start the control session at the bottom of the page.
droneConfig.modeFootnoteBciCloud: Select a model trained in the NeuraFlow app, start the deployment, then fly using the BCI signal. The session starts once the deployment is in the running state.
droneConfig.modeFootnoteBciLocalBridge: The model and inference run in your local bridge. When the WebSocket is connected, you can start — no model list in the web app and no cloud deployment.
droneConfig.modeFootnoteManual: Manual mode: fly with on-screen arrows — no BCI model or deployment. Extra settings stay lightweight so you can jump into a session quickly after what you set up below.

camera.label: Camera
camera.noSignalHint: Start DJI Fly RTMP to the MAVLink bridge local URL to enable live video.

droneHub.bciDescription: Pick a model, deploy it, and run a BCI session.
droneHub.manualDescription: Manual flying with on-screen arrow controls. No BCI model.

droneManual.configPlaceholder: On-screen arrow control settings will appear here — no BCI required.
droneManual.startHint: You can start a session immediately — no BCI model deployment.
droneManual.title: Manual control

dronePage.body: Drone control and related BCI features.
dronePage.title: Drone control

hub.carDescription: Drive an RC car from the NeuraFlow interface.
hub.subtitle: Pick a vehicle. Movement and focus exercises (BCI) live in separate areas.
hub.title: Vehicle control

localBridgePanel.subtitle: Inference on your machine. Load the model in the bridge app — the web app only opens the WebSocket link.
localBridgePanel.title: Local model (bridge)

mainBridgePanel.subtitle: Connect the bridge application and start the EEG stream before you begin the session.
mainBridgePanel.title: Main bridge (Cyton)

videoBridgePanel.djiHint: DJI Fly streams to your computer first; the MAVLink bridge relays video to NeuraFlow.
videoBridgePanel.startHint: Connect the MAVLink bridge and authorize video streaming.
videoBridgePanel.stepConnect: Click Connect and sign in to NeuraFlow in the browser.
videoBridgePanel.stepDjiFly: In DJI Fly, set custom RTMP to the URL shown in the MAVLink Bridge app.
videoBridgePanel.stepMavlink: Run the MAVLink Bridge desktop app on this computer.
videoBridgePanel.subtitle: Link DJI O4 camera feed for live view in the control panel.
videoBridgePanel.timeoutHint: Timed out. Complete login in the browser and ensure MAVLink Bridge is running.
videoBridgePanel.title: Drone video (DJI Fly)
